message:
feat(latency): add cross-domain blast-radius analysis with dependency DAG

Implement fault cascading analysis that models how failures propagate
across fault isolation domains via a directed dependency graph.

New types and components:
- DomainDependency: weighted edge (source -> target) with cascade_weight
  (0-100) representing deterministic propagation thresholds
- BlastRadiusReport: analysis output with origin, direct_risk (one-hop),
  transitive_risk (multi-hop BFS), and total_at_risk count
- BlastRadiusAnalyzer: stateless analyzer operating on a static edge set
  - default_graph() provides sensible defaults: Io->Storage (80),
    Scheduler->Budget (60), Recovery->Scheduler (40)
  - analyze() performs BFS reachability from the faulted domain to
    identify all directly and transitively impacted domains
- FaultIsolationManager integration: embeds BlastRadiusAnalyzer and
  exposes blast_radius_for() for on-demand analysis queries
- BlastRadiusPolicy: policy layer with configurable max_cascade_depth
  and per-domain overrides for custom risk tolerance
- PreemptiveIsolationAction: actionable output (isolate, throttle,
  monitor) with target domain, reason, and urgency level

Enables the ARS to understand cascading failure risk and take preemptive
isolation measures before damage propagates across domains.

+751 lines of blast-radius analysis infrastructure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
